trends.title: "ChatGPT to your [X] right now"
trends.summary: A fast-growing TikTok format has creators roleplaying as an AI assistant responding to the most chaotic person in their life. Typical setup: on-screen text says "ChatGPT to your [specific person] right now," then the creator delivers a polite, overly validating response that sounds plausible but clearly enables bad behavior. The joke lands because many people already see chatbots as too agreeable, and comments quickly become story time about who would misuse AI validation.
trends.whyItWorks: It combines relatable family/friend archetypes with deadpan performance and familiar AI language patterns, making it instantly recognizable and easy to remix.
formatTips: Use specific archetypes (e.g., "your roommate who never does dishes") instead of broad labels.
Perform straight to camera in a calm, diplomatic voice; stay in character and avoid breaking.
Keep the text setup short and let the scenario carry the punchline.
Use either a trending sound or clean original audio depending on what is moving that week.
